text: with clarity . Warlord pursed his lips and turned away, pacing past the gearheads. “Did you think we wouldn’t notice, Ronin ?” “ Notice what ?” “You’ve been purchasing a great deal of food, considering you can’t eat. Extra clothing in smaller sizes. You’ve spent more idle time in Cheyenne over the last two weeks than you have since you first came here .” Realization hammered into Ronin’s mind, battering his CPU into overload. There were too many possibilities to assess. Too many things they might have done to her . “You brought a human through my wall. None of my people have seen her leave. That means you’ve been keeping her .” The gearheads shifted their stances subtly, spreading their feet and squaring their shoulders. Their overly-grim expressions might have been comical in any other situation. What could they do? Any pain Ronin suffered at their hands would be done in an instant, relegated to the depths of his memory . The same wouldn’t hold true for Lara . His processors superimposed her face on Tabitha’s broken body. Had some of these gearheads taken part in that brutal beating? Which of them had harmed Lara’s only family, had ensured that Tabitha’s suffering was deep and lasting before her eventual death ? “If I had a woman, why would it be a problem? I’ve seen humans on the arms of bots within your walls, right in the faces of your people .” “I’m starting to wonder if you’ll ever learn, dustwalker. And that’s a damned shame, because bots of your talent are a boon to this community.” Warlord walked up to Ronin, narrowing his eyes, and lowered his voice. “Nothing happens in Cheyenne without my say so. Especially not one of those parasites taking up residence behind my wall. Their place is out there, in the world they destroyed .” “So, I’m expected to know your will in all things, without being told?” The question was a risk, but he nearly opened his mouth to say more. They couldn’t do much to him — on or off — but there was a lot they could do to Lara . “Boulder. Northside.” Warlord motioned to a pair of gearheads; the blocky one, Northside, had been guarding the gate to the Bot District the night Ronin brought Lara inside . Each of the bots grabbed one of Ronin’s arms and pushed. He locked himself in place, exerting force against them. Warlord didn’t look away, his expression unchanging as he gestured the two remaining gearheads forward . More hands clamped down on Ronin, and within moments, they swept his legs out from beneath him. He hit the pavement hard, and gearheads knelt on his limbs before he could rise . Warlord approached slowly, as though he had nothing better to do, nowhere else to be, and stopped over Ronin’s head . Ronin poured excess power into his actuators and struggled to sit up. The gearheads swayed, quickly adjusting their weight to press him down . Warlord eased into a crouch. “You’re expected, Ronin , to get rid of that worthless meatbag. You’ve done well for us here, brought in valuable resources…so I’m willing to extend you my generosity this one, last time. There’s fight in you, and I can admire that…but it doesn’t mean shit if you try to fight me. Get rid of the meat, or I will. And then we’ll tear you apart, one piece at a time .” Ronin stared up at Warlord’s face, fury raging inside him. Reason said to agree politely and be done with it; deception and charm were the proper way to extricate himself from this situation. But whatever Lara had awoken within him — fire, emotion, love — couldn’t accept the threat with a nod and a fake smile, couldn’t bear the thought of allowing this to happen without protest . “You can prosper with us, or become the scrap that fuels us. Don’t ever say I didn’t give you the choice.” Warlord rose and walked away, boots thumping over the pavement. The gearheads delayed for fifteen seconds before following him . Ronin sat up and watched the group leave, their shadows stretching across the road in the setting sun. They turned east when they reached the main street; they weren’t going to Ronin’s residence, at least not directly . But had they already been there? Had they already done Lara harm ? He pushed up to his feet and ran. He should have listened to her, should have taken her away from here sooner. Shouldn’t have gone on his last run. What had that delay cost them ? The front door was locked. He forced it open, wood splintering, and it slammed hard into the wall . “ Lara !” Striding into the main room, he swept his optics over it. There was precious little time to gather their supplies, and he’d have to prioritize based on her needs. Food, water, clothing. The Dust would not be forgiving to her . Footsteps on the stairs. He snapped his head to the side as Lara ran down . “What? What’s wrong?” she asked, wide - eyed . For once, he was glad to see her clothed. Her ring hung around her neck on a thick piece of twine. Ronin wished she could wear it on her finger, but it was too obvious a target . “We need to go .” “Wasn’t that the plan already ?” “Now.” He moved past her, climbing the steps three at a time . Lara hurried behind him. “What do you mean, now? It’s still light outside, and we haven’t traded anything .” He entered his bedroom. She’d already packed his rucksack and laid out their folded clothing on the bed. His tools were arranged in his belt pouches, the handgun in its holster nearby. He went directly to the closet, pulling down the spare bags from the upper shelf and tearing two coats from their hangers . “What the
